function.arguments:
{"code": "import json
import re

# Load the business data from file
file_path = 'var_functions.query_db:12'
if isinstance(file_path, str) and '.json' in file_path:
    with open('/mnt/data/var_functions.query_db_12.json', 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:12', [])

print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))
print()
print('Sample business:')
print(businesses[0] if businesses else 'No data')
print()

# Extract state and review count per business
state_reviews = {}
business_state_map = {}

for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    business_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    review_count = int(biz.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Extract state abbreviation (2 capital letters) from description
    match = re.search(r', ([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count
        business_state_map[business_id] = state
    else:
        # Check for state at the end of the line
        match2 = re.search(r'in ([A-Z]{2})$', desc)
        if match2:
            state = match2.group(1)
            state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count
            business_state_map[business_id] = state

print('States with review counts (top 10):')
sorted_states = sorted(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
for state, count in sorted_states[:10]:
    print(f'  {state}: {count} reviews')
print()

if sorted_states:
    top_state = sorted_states[0][0]
    top_state_reviews = sorted_states[0][1]
    print(f'Top state: {top_state} with {top_state_reviews} total reviews')
    
    result = {
        'top_state': top_state,
        'top_state_total_reviews': top_state_reviews,
        'state_counts': sorted_states[:10],
        'business_count': len(businesses)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No state data found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}

}

{"code": "import json
import re

# Get businesses data
businesses = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:74', [])
print('Number of businesses:', len(businesses))

# Process a few businesses to see the pattern
for i, biz in enumerate(businesses[:3]):
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    print(f'Business {i+1}: {desc[:80]}...')
    
    # Try different regex patterns
    match1 = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    match2 = re.search(r'\bin\s+([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    print(f'  Pattern 1 match: {match1.group(1) if match1 else None}')
    print(f'  Pattern 2 match: {match2.group(1) if match2 else None}')

# Now process all businesses properly
state_reviews = {}
state_businesses = {}

for biz in businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    review_count = int(biz.get('review_count', 0))
    
    # Look for state in description
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_reviews[state] = state_reviews.get(state, 0) + review_count
        state_businesses[state] = state_businesses.get(state, 0) + 1
        print(f'Found: {state} - {biz_id} - {review_count} reviews')

print('State totals:', state_reviews)"}

var_functions.query_db:20.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
